title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
Combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
Order the Upload Accessible Feedback S3, CloudFront, and Go storage-delivery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Upload Accessible Feedback.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Upload Accessible Feedback assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Upload Accessible Feedback, keep Upload Accessible Feedback connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 1188 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Upload Accessible Feedback has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-upload-accessible-feedback-m01dfh" that uses case 6315 to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
